# figura

Claude Code skill for publication-quality figures, plots, and diagrams. Render-view-fix iteration loop catches visual defects at print size before submission.

![Torus example](docs/torus.png)

*Hero image generated by `skills/figura/examples/torus.py` ‚Äî single-column square, viridis surface shaded by depth, Helvetica embedded as TrueType in the PDF. Example outputs under `skills/figura/figures/` are gitignored; only this README copy lives in `docs/`.*

## Install

Add the marketplace in Claude Code:

```
/plugin marketplace add chrischoy/figura
```

Install the plugin:

```
/plugin install figura@figura
```

Reload plugins:

```
/reload-plugins
```

The skill auto-triggers on phrases like "figure for my paper", "plot for the manuscript", "architecture diagram", "publication-quality", "submission-quality figure", or any reference to LaTeX, NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, IEEE, ACM, Nature, Science, or arXiv.

The plugin also exposes four slash commands:

| Command | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| `/figura:iterate <script.py>` | Runs the render ‚Üí view ‚Üí fix loop on a figure script. Caps at two cycles. |
| `/figura:beautify <script.py>` | Upgrades a "default matplotlib"-looking figure to publication style (fonts, palette, spines, vector export). |
| `/figura:fix-overlap <script.py>` | Targeted collision fixer ‚Äî tick labels, legend, multi-panel, panel labels. |
| `/figura:analyze-image <image>` | Read-only visual audit. Reports defects by category and severity; does not modify anything. |

If you prefer to install by hand, clone the repo into your skills directory:

```
git clone https://github.com/chrischoy/figura ~/.claude/skills/figura-src
ln -s ~/.claude/skills/figura-src/skills/figura ~/.claude/skills/figura
/reload-skills
```

After install, install the Python deps in your host environment (Claude Code does not manage Python). Clone the repo and install from there ‚Äî the plugin install path is implementation-defined and not a stable place to invoke pip from:

```
git clone https://github.com/chrischoy/figura /tmp/figura
pip install -r /tmp/figura/requirements.txt
pip install -r /tmp/figura/requirements-extras.txt   # optional
```

For graphviz diagrams, also install the `dot` binary (`brew install graphviz` / `apt install graphviz`).

One simple first run, inside Claude Code:

```
Make a publication-quality 3D plot of a torus
```

Claude renders `figures/fig_torus.{pdf,svg,png}` in your working directory and views the PNG to verify legibility at print size. The matching script also lives at `skills/figura/examples/torus.py` for direct execution.

## Layout

```
.claude-plugin/
  marketplace.json         ‚Äî declares this repo as a single-plugin marketplace
  plugin.json              ‚Äî plugin metadata
commands/
  iterate.md               ‚Äî /figura:iterate ‚Äî render‚Üíview‚Üífix loop
  beautify.md              ‚Äî /figura:beautify ‚Äî upgrade to pub style
  fix-overlap.md           ‚Äî /figura:fix-overlap ‚Äî targeted collision fix
  analyze-image.md         ‚Äî /figura:analyze-image ‚Äî read-only audit
skills/
  figura/
    SKILL.md               ‚Äî entry point; loaded into Claude's context
    scripts/
      pubstyle.py          ‚Äî publication rcParams (fonts, spines, vector-safe)
      colors.py            ‚Äî colorblind-safe palettes (Okabe-Ito, Tol Muted)
      export.py            ‚Äî atomic multi-format save (PDF + SVG + PNG)
    references/
      plots.md             ‚Äî line, bar, scatter, heatmap, violin, multi-panel,
                             histogram, ablation, 3D surface
      diagrams.md          ‚Äî matplotlib / graphviz / hand-SVG patterns
      iteration.md         ‚Äî render‚Üíview‚Üífix loop + defect catalog
      checklist.md         ‚Äî pre-submission QA
    examples/
      torus.py             ‚Äî runnable end-to-end 3D surface example
    figures/               ‚Äî example outputs
README.md
requirements.txt           ‚Äî core Python deps (host env, not Claude Code)
requirements-extras.txt    ‚Äî optional deps for diagram/SVG/scipy patterns
```

## Quick start (without Claude Code)

```python
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "<path-to-repo>/skills/figura/scripts")

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pubstyle, colors, export

pubstyle.apply()
colors.apply_cycle()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=pubstyle.figsize("single"))
ax.plot([1, 2, 3], [1, 4, 2])
ax.set_xlabel("x"); ax.set_ylabel("y")

export.save(fig, "fig_demo")    # writes ./figures/fig_demo.{pdf,svg,png}
```

For venue-specific defaults: `pubstyle.apply(venue="ieee")` (also `neurips`, `icml`, `iclr`, `acm`, `nature`).

## What it fixes

Defaults matplotlib ships with are designed for screen exploration, not print. This skill fixes the most common "default matplotlib" tells:

- Embedded TrueType fonts in PDFs (`pdf.fonttype = 42`) ‚Äî journals reject Type 3.
- Helvetica/Arial body text + matching `stixsans` math glyphs.
- Top/right spines off, subtle ticks, no gridlines by default.
- Colorblind-safe categorical palette (Okabe-Ito) + perceptually uniform sequential (`viridis`/`cividis`) and diverging (`RdBu_r`) recommendations.
- Print-size figure dimensions baked into the helpers, so 8 pt fonts stay 8 pt.
- `export.save()` writes PDF + SVG + PNG together with `bbox_inches="tight"`, atomically (temp + replace), with path-traversal guard on the name.

## Iteration loop

Render ‚Üí view PNG at native resolution ‚Üí list defects ‚Üí apply fixes from `references/iteration.md` catalog ‚Üí re-render. Cap at two cycles. The PNG renders at 300 DPI from a figure whose dimensions are the actual print size, so on-screen pixels match what readers see on paper.
